title: Flaky Venezuelan Maple Crumble
description: A cozy, herbal crumble that blends Venezuelan plantains with sweet maple syrup for a flaky, comforting dessert.

ingredients.name: coconut oil (or butter)
ingredients.notes: Use coconut oil for vegan version, butter for richer flavor.

instructions: Preheat the oven to 180 °C (350 °F) and grease a 9×9‑inch baking pan.
Place the plantains in a large bowl and mash until smooth. Stir in maple syrup, cinnamon, ginger, cloves, dried cranberries, and salt until well combined.
In a separate bowl, mix the rolled oats, almond flour, and coconut oil. Using a fork or whisk, disperse the oil to form a crumbly mixture.
Sprinkle half of the crumb mixture over the plantain filling.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
Remove the crumble from the oven, let it rest for 10 minutes, then serve warm, optionally topped with a dollop of coconut cream or whipped cream.
Enjoy your flaky, herbal Venezuelan maple crumble as a comforting dessert.
